title: Aigent Nakamoto — Chat History & Persona-Scoped Memory Specification
purpose: Replication-grade specification of how chat history is captured, scoped to active persona (Qripto / KNYT / Anon), enriched with metadata, summarized, and replayed as conversational context. Designed for re-implementation against AS-API identity infrastructure.

operational_model.overview: Chat history is a persona-aware, append-only event log of user↔agent exchanges. Each row is one Q/A pair tagged with an interaction_type (agent channel) plus a metadata JSONB envelope carrying the active persona, conversation ID, model used, knowledge sources, and citations. RLS guarantees per-user isolation. A sliding-window memory cache (last 8 exchanges) plus periodic LLM summarization (every 10 interactions) provides bounded long-term recall.
lifecycle: 1. User sends a prompt in an agent interface (Aigent / Learn / Earn / Connect).
2. Active persona is read from localStorage key 'selected-iqube' (values: 'Qripto', 'KNYT', or null→'Anon').
3. usePersonaContext hook resolves persona blakQube fields → contextual system-prompt fragment.
4. prepareConversationContext() retrieves prior summaries + checks if summarization threshold (10 unsummarized) is reached.
5. AigentConversationService loads sliding-window memory (last 8 exchanges) for the conversationId.
6. Edge function (aigent-ai / learn-ai / etc.) is invoked with: prompt + persona context + sliding window + historical summaries.
7. Response is returned to the client and rendered via MessageContent (raw markdown/HTML preserved).
8. storeUserInteraction() inserts a row into public.user_interactions with enriched metadata envelope.
9. React Query invalidates ['user-interactions', userId, interactionType] → UI refreshes history.
10. When unsummarized count ≥ 10, summarize-history edge function is triggered → conversation_summaries row written, source rows marked summarized=true.

scoping_rules.by_user: RLS: auth.uid() = user_id on every CRUD. No cross-user access possible.
scoping_rules.by_persona: Persona is NOT a column — it is stored in metadata.activePersona. UI filters in-memory when interactionType is 'qripto', 'knyt', or 'all'. The interaction_type column ('learn'|'earn'|'connect'|'aigent') represents the agent CHANNEL, not the persona.
scoping_rules.by_conversation: metadata.conversationId (UUID) groups exchanges within one chat thread. Generated client-side via crypto.randomUUID() if absent.
scoping_rules.legacy_compatibility: Aigent reads merge native interaction_type='aigent' rows with legacy interaction_type='learn' rows whose metadata.agentType==='aigent' (migration support).

primary_table.name: public.user_interactions
primary_table.purpose: Append-only log of every user↔agent exchange across all four agent channels.
primary_table.ddl:
CREATE TABLE public.user_interactions (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id uuid NOT NULL,
  query text NOT NULL,
  response text NOT NULL,
  interaction_type text NOT NULL,
  metadata jsonb,
  summarized boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
ALTER TABLE public.user_interactions ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

columns.id: uuid PK — exchange identifier; used to derive client-side message IDs ('{id}-user', '{id}-agent').
columns.user_id: uuid — auth.uid() of the user; never references auth.users with FK (per Supabase guidance).
columns.query: text — the raw user prompt as submitted (no preprocessing).
columns.response: text — the raw agent response, preserves markdown/HTML/citations for replay.
columns.summarized: boolean — flipped to true once included in a conversation_summaries row.

rls_policies.name: Users can view their own interactions
Users can create their own interactions
Users can update their own interactions
Users can delete their own interactions

recommended_indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_user_interactions_user_type_created ON public.user_interactions(user_id, interaction_type, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_interactions_unsummarized ON public.user_interactions(user_id, interaction_type) WHERE summarized = false;
CREATE INDEX idx_user_interactions_metadata_gin ON public.user_interactions USING GIN (metadata);

summary_table.name: public.conversation_summaries
summary_table.purpose: Compact LLM-generated rollups of older exchanges, used as long-term context injected into system prompts.

columns.summary_text: text — ≤250-word natural-language summary produced by gpt-4o-mini.
columns.included_interaction_ids: text[] — ids of user_interactions rows folded into this summary.

rls_policies.name: Users can view their own summaries
Users can create their own summaries
Users can update their own summaries
Users can delete their own summaries

summary_table.expiration_policy: SUMMARY_EXPIRATION_DAYS = 30 (defined in conversation-history.ts) — summaries older than 30 days SHOULD be excluded from prompt injection (currently advisory; not enforced via DB).

session_table.name: public.user_sessions
session_table.purpose: Coarse session lifecycle tracking (login/logout, device info). Independent of conversation threads.

chat_session_table.name: public.chat_sessions
chat_session_table.purpose: Optional per-user chat session container (last_message_at + session_data jsonb). Used for higher-level grouping; the source of truth for individual exchanges remains user_interactions.

metadata_envelope.description: JSONB shape stored in user_interactions.metadata. Enriched at write-time by storeUserInteraction() and at response-time by the edge function.

conversationId.source: crypto.randomUUID() — stable across a chat thread

agentType.purpose: legacy disambiguator when interaction_type='learn' but logical agent is Aigent

knowledgeSource.example: iQube + COYN + Qripto + metaKnyts Knowledge Bases

historicResponse.purpose: Marks the message as loaded from DB (not freshly generated).

agentTheme.purpose: Theme key used by MessageContent for styling.

rawContent.purpose: Tells MessageContent to apply sanitization rather than markdown parsing.

example.knowledgeSource: iQube + COYN + Qripto + metaKnyts Knowledge Bases

sliding_window.description: Last 8 exchanges (≈4 user/agent pairs) per conversationId, fetched via metadata.conversationId match.

context_budget.strategy: truncate oldest exchanges first when budget exceeded

long_term_summarization.trigger_logic: checkIfSummarizationNeeded() counts WHERE summarized=false AND metadata->>conversationId = :id; fires when ≥10.
post_actions: Insert one conversation_summaries row with included_interaction_ids[]
PATCH each source user_interactions row to summarized=true
long_term_summarization.prompt_template: You are a helpful AI that summarizes conversations. Create a concise summary (maximum 250 words) of the following conversation between a user and an AI assistant about {agentType}-related topics. Focus on the key questions, insights, and takeaways. This summary will be used as context for future conversations.

context_injection.output_format:
<conversation-history>
The user has had previous conversations with you. Here are summaries of past interactions:

Summary 1 ({date}): {summary_text}

...
</conversation-history>
context_injection.appended_to: system prompt of the next agent invocation

responsibilities: Read 'selected-iqube' from localStorage to determine active persona.
Fetch matching blakQube row (qripto_personas or knyt_personas) for current auth.uid().
Build a ConversationContext object: { isAnonymous, personaType, displayName, profession, web3Interests, ... }.
Listen to qryptoPersonaActivated/Deactivated and knytPersonaActivated/Deactivated DOM events for real-time refresh.
Debounce refreshes (200ms) and gate them behind NavigationGuard to prevent compilation cascades.
persona_context_layer.context_injection_into_prompt: PersonaContextService.generateContextualPrompt(context, topic) returns a markdown block summarizing the persona that is concatenated to the system prompt. Anonymous users get a generic greeting block.

hooks.name: useMessageHistory
hooks.responsibility: Convert raw user_interactions rows into AgentMessage[] (one user msg + one agent msg per row), sort chronologically, and merge with initialMessages.

agent_message.metadata: { ...interaction.metadata, historicResponse: true, agentTheme: agentType, rawContent: true }

history_loading_pipeline_client.cache_busting: On load, useMessageHistory clears sessionStorage and localStorage entries matching `${agentType}_conversation_cache_${user.id}` to drop any corrupted prior caches.

steps: 1. Resolve user_id via supabase.auth.getSession() if not provided.
4. INSERT INTO user_interactions (query, response, interaction_type, metadata, user_id) RETURNING *.
6. Caller (e.g. useAigent) invalidates the React Query cache.
write_path_pipeline.alternate_entry: src/services/agent-service.ts contains a fallback direct-insert path used by edge-function-driven flows.

agent_inference.name: aigent-ai
agent_inference.purpose: Crypto-Agentic AI for the Aigent Nakamoto channel; multiplexes OpenAI vs Venice based on veniceActivated flag.

memory_management.name: summarize-history
memory_management.purpose: Rolls up unsummarized exchanges into a conversation_summaries row.
memory_management.auth: Service role; called by triggerConversationSummarize from the client.

history_migration.name: naka-history-migrate
history_migration.purpose: One-shot migration utility to backfill metadata.agentType on legacy 'learn' rows that should be 'aigent'.

security_model.authorization: RLS-only — every read/write is gated by auth.uid() = user_id. No service-role calls from the client.
isolation: Row-level: a user can never see another user's interactions or summaries.
Persona-level: filtering by activePersona happens client-side; persona is not a cryptographic boundary, only an organizational tag within a user's own data.
security_model.service_role_use: Only the summarize-history edge function uses SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (to bypass RLS for the summarize+mark-summarized atomic operation).
security_model.pii_handling: query and response columns may contain PII. They inherit RLS isolation. Persona PII (names, wallets, OM tier) lives in qripto_personas / knyt_personas — see persona-iqube-schema-spec.json.

prerequisites: AS-API issues a DID (decentralized identifier) per user.
A relational store (Postgres or equivalent) with row-level filtering by DID.
An LLM gateway capable of chat-completion calls (for inference + summarization).
steps: 1. Map AS-API DID → user_id (uuid). Use the DID as the partition key on every table.
2. Create equivalents of user_interactions and conversation_summaries with the schema above; replace 'auth.uid()' in policies with your DID-bearing claim.
3. Implement a persona resolver: read the active persona identifier from your client state and inject it as metadata.activePersona on every write.

4. Implement a write API matching storeUserInteraction (query, response, interaction_type, metadata). Always merge in { activePersona, timestamp, conversationId } server-side or in the client wrapper.
5. Implement a read API matching getUserInteractions with optional interaction_type filter. Return rows ordered by created_at DESC, limit/offset paginated.
6. Implement the sliding-window memory loader (last 8 rows by metadata.conversationId match). Include the legacy fallback strategies if you have pre-existing data.
7. Implement the summarization worker: every Nth interaction per (user, conversationId) where summarized=false, call your LLM with the summarizer prompt; persist the summary; flip summarized=true.
8. Implement getHistoricalContextForPrompt(agentType, k=3) — return top-k recent summaries formatted as <conversation-history>...</conversation-history>.
9. Concatenate persona context block + historical summaries block + sliding window transcript into the system prompt of every inference call.
10. On the client, use a query-cache library (React Query or equivalent) keyed by (user_id, interaction_type); invalidate after every write.
compatibility_notes: If you do not need multiple agent channels, fix interaction_type to a single value but keep the column for future expansion.
If your persona model has more than two personas, simply add new values to metadata.activePersona — no schema change required.
metadata is JSONB on Postgres. On other stores, use a JSON-typed column with a GIN-equivalent index for containment queries.
